name: ringback
displayName: ringback — your agent calls your phone
description: Two-way voice phone calls plus tiered alerts for your AI agent, over free self-hosted SIP. The agent rings your phone and talks to you when it needs a decision, and it is presence-aware so it only calls when you are away from the keyboard. Runs the engine in Docker, so there is no local build.

sip_id.title: Your SIP address
sip_id.description: Your Linphone SIP address, e.g. sip:you@sip.linphone.org. This is also the phone the agent calls unless you set a different number below.

sip_user.title: SIP username
sip_user.description: Your Linphone username (the part before the @).

sip_pass.title: SIP password
sip_pass.description: Your Linphone account password. Stored in your OS keychain, never in a file.

sip_callee.title: Address to call (optional)
sip_callee.description: SIP address the agent dials. Leave blank to call your own SIP address above.

display_name.title: Caller-ID name (optional)
display_name.description: Name shown on your phone when the agent calls, e.g. My Claude.

stun_server.title: STUN server
stun_server.description: Used to discover the container's public address so two-way audio works through Docker's NAT. The default works for everyone; change it only if you prefer another (e.g. stun.linphone.org).

alert_channel.title: Alert channel (optional)
alert_channel.description: Push backend for ringback-alert: ntfy, pushover, or both. Leave the default if you only want voice calls.

ntfy_url.title: ntfy topic URL (optional)
ntfy_url.description: Full ntfy URL with a long, random topic, e.g. https://ntfy.sh/your-random-topic. Anyone who knows the topic can read it, so make it hard to guess.

ntfy_token.title: ntfy token (optional)
ntfy_token.description: Only needed for a self-hosted ntfy with auth. Leave blank for public ntfy.sh.

pushover_token.title: Pushover app token (optional)
pushover_token.description: App token from pushover.net (gives true iOS Critical Alerts).
